hypothesis: Higher CIS security compliance correlates with higher idle CPU/RAM
evidence: Security vs Idle CPU: ρ=0.894, p=0.0405
Security vs Idle RAM: ρ=0.894, p=0.0405
k0s per-container cgroup validation: idle orchestration tax = 0.380% of node capacity

hypothesis: System overhead grows under load, consuming resources that reduce scheduling capacity
evidence: Idle CPU vs svc-create correlation: ρ=0.300, p=0.6238

k0s cgroup validation:
  System CPU idle: 1.52%
  System CPU under CP heavy: 14.02%
  Growth factor: 9.2×
  → System overhead consumes 9× more CPU under load, reducing resources for workload scheduling

Extreme case: KubeEdge (highest overhead, 6.8% idle CPU)
  120-pod latency: 33800 ms vs k3s: 5800 ms
  KubeEdge is 5.8× slower at scale

hypothesis: Lightweight distros have lower pod-startup latency
evidence: Pod startup latency — Lightweight vs Heavy:
Heavy (k8s, KubeEdge, OpenYurt) mean median: 5089 ms
CP Heavy pod-create — Lightweight mean: 21.4 ms, Heavy mean: 20.1 ms (U=80, p=0.6198)
Tail latency (max) — Lightweight mean: 3667 ms, Heavy mean: 26667 ms
KubeEdge max latency outliers: mean=70567 ms, max=143000 ms

hypothesis: Higher efficiency enables equal workload at lower cost
evidence:
k0s cgroup DP Redis Density:
  System CPU: 1.16%
  Workload CPU: 206.11%
  Workload share: 99.4% of total
  → Low system overhead allows workload to consume nearly all available CPU
Key comparison: k3s (11467 ops/%CPU) vs k8s (3654 ops/%CPU) vs KubeEdge (699 ops/%CPU)

hypothesis: Higher security compliance correlates with longer setup time
evidence: Security score vs setup time: ρ=0.918, p=0.0280
k3s (7.21% CIS) — single binary, 2h setup, minimal configuration
k8s (55.0% CIS) — multiple components, 5h setup, extensive tuning
KubeEdge (55.0% CIS) — complex cloud-edge architecture, 14h setup
Note: KubeEdge/OpenYurt high setup time comes from edge complexity,
not just security. Confounding factor: edge features ≈ security burden

hypothesis: Lower resource footprint correlates with weaker security
evidence: Idle CPU overhead vs security: ρ=0.894, p=0.0405
Idle RAM overhead vs security: ρ=0.894, p=0.0405
Key insight: k3s achieves lowest resource footprint by stripping security features (embedded SQLite vs etcd, no default RBAC hardening), resulting in the lowest CIS score (7.21%).
k0s strikes a middle ground: moderate resources (23.69% CIS) with single-binary simplicity.
